LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STOCKBRIDGE CAPITAL GROUP LLC NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. COURT FINDS THAT (1) ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS SEPARABLE AND NOT SUBJECT TO THE CITED CONDITION PRECEDENT; (2) DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO ATTACH THE AAA RULES DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT MEET THE CLEAR AND UNMISTAKABLE STANDARD RE AN ARBITRATION DECIDING ARBITRABILITY; (4) THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS UNCONSCIONABLE; AND (5) THE UNCONSCIONABLE PROVISIONS CANNOT BE SEVERED. PARTIES TO SUBMIT LETTER BRIEFS TO THE COURT BY MARCH 18, 2011 REGARDING THE REQUEST FOR A STATEMENT OF DECISION. IF A STATEMENT OF DECISION IS REQUIRED, THE COURT WILL REQUEST THAT PLAINTIFF SUBMIT A PROPOSED STATEMENT OF DECISION. (302/PHA) JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO; REPORTER: GORDON AIAVAO, CSR #11216
NOTICE OF AUTOMATIC STAY OF PROCEEDINGS PENDING APPEAL FILED BY DEFENDANT STOCKBRIDGE CAPITAL GROUP LLC

REMITTITUR DISMISSED (A131670 DIV 5)

Ntc And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STOCKBRIDGE CAPITAL GROUP LLC NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY. Appearances: Christopher B. Hockett of Davis Polk (650.752.2009) for defendant and moving party; Alan B. Exelrod of Rudy Exelrod Zieff & Lowe (434.9800) for plaintiff and responding party; Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Court finds that (1) arbitration agreement is separable and not subject to the cited condition precedent; (2) defendant's failure to attach the AAA rules demonstrates that defendant did not meet the clear and unmistakable standard re an arbitration deciding arbitrability; (4) the arbitration clause is unconscionable; and (5) the unconscionable provisions cannot be severed. Parties to submit letter briefs to the court by March 18, 2011 regarding the request for a statement of decision. If a statement of decision is required, the court will request that plaintiff submit a proposed statement of decision. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216
